description: for the asset selection logic in room editor, when the user space bars to the "boundary asset" selection with space bar, the user should be able to select boundary assets that are rendered via the dynamic render system. able to select indavidual asset (just right click no left click (we can only open asset info ui for it). these boundary assets should also be able to be selected when the user is in "all" selection mode. additionaly make sure the map wide assets are not able to be selected unless map assets mode or all is selected make sure theya re not selectable in other modes

Completely overhaul the tags section in the asset info UI. Display tags in green and anti-tags in red. Below them, show a list of recommended tags in yellow. Add a search bar at the bottom that dynamically updates the recommended list as the user types. Always include a single orange tag at the top of the recommended list that exactly matches the current search input, unless it already exists as a tag or anti-tag. Users must be able to drag tags between all lists (green, red, yellow) in any direction. Double-clicking a tag removes it from its current list. Double-clicking a recommended tag hides it until the panel is reopened. All UI interactions must correctly update and persist the underlying data. Codex must review the attached image to understand the intended layout and behavior before implementing.

Clean up the animation editor workflow. Remove the geometry and animation dropdowns since they are unused. Remove the anchors button from the geometry dropdown. Remove the configure animations button from the asset info UI. The animation editor should open via tab navigation and appear immediately after the asset info panel. When opened, it must be full screen with no docked asset info panel visible. Do not display the frame or animation navigator while the animation editor is open.

While clicking and dragging an asset, do not render or highlight anchor points for any other assets. Anchor visibility and hover effects must be restricted to the selected asset only. Restore normal behavior once the asset is no longer being dragged.

Refactor the depth effects panel layout to be more compact. Place the preview images side by side with their corresponding slider controls so that both controls and both previews exist on a single row. Ensure all buttons remain visible at the bottom of the panel. Scrolling must not be required. Reorganize and resize elements to fit cleanly within the available space. Codex must inspect the attached screenshot to understand the current layout issues before implementing changes.

Attack hitbox system is completely broken. It does not render in the editor, is not saved on close, and does not exist at runtime after being added. Assume both hitbox and attack box systems are affected. Codex must analyze the attached screenshot to understand the issue, then trace the full pipeline including creation, rendering, serialization, and runtime loading. Identify and fix the root cause so hitboxes function correctly in both editor and runtime.

Movement panel in the asset info editing stack is not functioning. No movement points are displayed and existing movement data is not loaded. Codex must analyze the attached screenshot to understand the issue, then investigate the full pipeline including loading, rendering, editing, and saving. Ensure each animation frame always has default movement data of zero if none exists, and that these points are visible and editable. Fix data formatting and persistence so movement works correctly in the editor, saved data, and runtime.
